summary: Bootstrap scan ran `mvn -B -ntp test` successfully; 398 tests passed with 0 failures or errors.
Detected documentation drift: benchmark jar/tag examples use 1.3.0 while pom.xml declares 1.0.0, and MIGRATION.md/RELEASE.md still describe SQL-like subqueries plus multi-join plans as unsupported.
Initial /ai memory scaffold and indexes created from repository scan of code, tests, CI, scripts, and markdown docs.

Extended WP5 selective materialization so computed-field queries can keep lazy/selective single-join parent and child materialization when schemas remain collision-free; verified with full `mvn -q test` plus focused regression coverage.

Added `HotspotMicroJmhBenchmark.computedFieldJoinSelectiveMaterialization`, wired it into the hotspot suite/docs, corrected `docs/benchmarking.md` to the current `1.0.0` benchmark jar examples, and verified with `mvn -q test`, doc consistency, and a quick JMH smoke run.

Built the forked benchmark runner and captured the first `-prof gc` numbers for `HotspotMicroJmhBenchmark.computedFieldJoinSelectiveMaterialization`: about 37.9 us/op and 363,824 B/op at size=1000, and about 361.5 us/op and 3,531,826 B/op at size=10000. No threshold was promoted yet.

Added end-to-end computed-field join comparison coverage to `PojoLensJoinJmhBenchmark`, locked it with `PojoLensJoinJmhBenchmarkParityTest`, documented the local diagnostic command in `docs/benchmarking.md`, and captured the first forked `-prof gc` comparison: PojoLens at about 0.335 ms/op and 2,138,298 B/op for size=1000 plus 3.750 ms/op and 20,981,581 B/op for size=10000, versus the manual baseline at about 0.009 ms/op / 84,512 B/op and 0.097 ms/op / 927,128 B/op.

Promoted `PojoLensJoinJmhBenchmark.pojoLensJoinLeftComputedField` into the strict core benchmark suite after repeated warm and cold runs. The end-to-end path is now listed in `scripts/benchmark-suite-main.args`, documented in `docs/benchmarking.md`, and budgeted in `benchmarks/thresholds.json` at 150 ms/op for size=1000 and 500 ms/op for size=10000. A second forked `-prof gc` run remained near 0.335 ms/op / 2,138,338 B/op at 1000 and 3.589 ms/op / 20,981,552 B/op at 10000, while repeated cold guardrail runs stayed around 57.6-57.9 ms/op and 166.9-169.8 ms/op.

Validated the updated strict core suite end-to-end with `target/benchmarks-main-verify.json` plus `BenchmarkThresholdChecker --strict`. The computed-field join guardrail passed at about 109.706 ms/op for size=1000 and 160.863 ms/op for size=10000, so the 1k budget was widened from 150 ms/op to 250 ms/op while the 10k budget remained 500 ms/op.

Closed WP5 in `TODO.md` at the accepted selective/lazy scope boundary, keeping conservative fallbacks for multi-join, explicit rule-group, full-row-output, and collision cases as intentional stable behavior. Moved remaining benchmark-tightening work into new follow-up package WP14 and updated `/ai` state/handoff so future sessions do not treat WP5 as unfinished.

Captured a focused warm JFR for `PojoLensJoinJmhBenchmark.pojoLensJoinLeftComputedField` at size=10000 using `-wi 5 -i 10 -r 300ms`. The benchmark averaged about 5.505 ms/op versus about 0.148 ms/op for the manual baseline, and the profile showed heavy allocation churn dominated by `ComputedFieldSupport.materializeRow`, `JoinEngine.mergeFields`, `ReflectionUtil.collectQueryRowFieldTypes`, and `SqlExpressionEvaluator$Parser.*`. Rewrote `TODO.md` into an active backlog with new profiler-driven work packages and removed completed work-package sections.

Started WP14 by replacing per-evaluation numeric-expression parsing with cached compiled ASTs in `SqlExpressionEvaluator`, reusing compiled computed-field definitions inside `ComputedFieldSupport.materializeRows()`, and adding regression coverage in `SqlExpressionEvaluatorTest` plus `ComputedFieldRegistryTest`. Verified with focused tests and full `mvn -q test`.

Regenerated affected AI indexes after adding the new internal evaluator test and rerunning the suite; `ai/indexes/test-index.json` now records 406 tests and `ai/indexes/files-index.json` includes `SqlExpressionEvaluatorTest`.

Migrated the AI memory system to a two-tier hot/cold layout, rewrote root and /ai agent instructions to load only hot context by default, regenerated the five cold indexes, moved freshness tracking to ai/memory-state.json, and removed redundant legacy memory files.

Fixed process-document drift by replacing hardcoded benchmark jar examples in CONTRIBUTING.md, updating MIGRATION.md and RELEASE.md to reflect limited SQL-like subquery support plus chained joins, and expanding the doc-consistency scripts to check those invariants. Verified with scripts/check-doc-consistency.ps1.

Started WP15 by removing bulk QueryField cloning from ComputedFieldSupport and JoinEngine on the common selective-join path, adding multi-child computed join regression coverage, and verifying with full `mvn -q test` plus short JMH smokes: about 0.257 ms/op for `PojoLensJoinJmhBenchmark.pojoLensJoinLeftComputedField` at size=1000 and about 40.405 us/op / 363,856 B/op for `HotspotMicroJmhBenchmark.computedFieldJoinSelectiveMaterialization` at size=1000.

Started WP16 by replacing the post-join `collectQueryRowFieldTypes(rows)` rescan with derived join schema propagation from FilterQueryBuilder join metadata, adding unmatched-left-join field-type regression coverage, and verifying with full `mvn -q test` plus a short 1k computed-field join smoke at about 0.249 ms/op.

Captured a warmed JFR and warmed manual baseline for `PojoLensJoinJmhBenchmark.pojoLensJoinLeftComputedField` at size=10000 using `-wi 5 -i 10 -r 300ms`. The end-to-end path improved to about 3.029 ms/op versus about 0.108 ms/op for the manual baseline, and the warmed JFR confirmed the old `SqlExpressionEvaluator$Parser.*` hot spots dropped out while `ComputedFieldSupport.materializeRow`, `ReflectionUtil.collectQueryRowFieldTypes`, `JoinEngine.mergeFields`, `ReflectionUtil.extractQueryFields`, and `ReflectionUtil.toDomainRows` remain the main repository hotspots.

Pushed WP15 further by replacing per-row computed-field value maps with a schema-aware materialization plan in `ComputedFieldSupport`, materializing joined rows in place through `FilterImpl.join()` / `FilterQueryBuilder.setMaterializedRows(...)`, and verifying with `mvn -q test` plus warmed JMH reruns: about 2.605 ms/op for `PojoLensJoinJmhBenchmark.pojoLensJoinLeftComputedField` at size=10000 versus the unchanged manual baseline around 0.108 ms/op.

Replaced the benchmark-shaped selective single-join hot path with `FastArrayQuerySupport`, added flattened array-row read/projection support in `ReflectionUtil`, wired `FilterImpl` / `FilterExecutionPlan` / `FilterQueryBuilder` to dispatch on schema-driven array execution, and added `FilterImplFastPathTest`. Verified with focused tests, full `mvn -q test`, a rebuilt `benchmark-runner` jar, warmed JMH at about 1.091 ms/op versus a current manual baseline around 0.150 ms/op at size=10000, and `-prof gc` at about 1.441 ms/op / 3,107,771 B/op.

Regenerated affected AI indexes and architecture memory after adding `FastArrayQuerySupport` and `FilterImplFastPathTest`; `files-index.json` now records 133 main Java files / 63 test Java files, `test-index.json` records a passing `mvn -q test` run with 410 tests, and `symbols-index.json` includes the new fast-path engine class.

Refined WP17 in `TODO.md` into an active highest-priority work package with an explicit combined CPU/allocation hotspot order, benchmark-driven guardrails, and concrete success thresholds (<0.75 ms/op near-term, <0.45 ms/op strong) for the selective single-join array path; refreshed hot AI state to match.

Started WP17 exploration with lower-overhead reflection access and more aggressive fast-path filtering/materialization ideas, but warmed `PojoLensJoinJmhBenchmark.pojoLensJoinLeftComputedField` regressed in repeated local reruns, so the code experiments were reverted. Focused tests remained green, and a reverted-code rerun still measured about 1.475 ms/op at size=10000, indicating the next WP17 step should re-establish a controlled benchmark/profile baseline before landing more code.

Continued WP17 with a controlled repro/profile loop, then landed two benchmark-backed optimizations on the selective single-join fast path: a specialized single-rule matcher in `FastArrayQuerySupport` and a narrower read-side field-path optimization in `ReflectionUtil`. Focused tests stayed green, the warmed 10k computed-field join rerun improved to about 1.043 ms/op versus the current manual baseline around 0.161 ms/op, and the latest `-prof gc` rerun measured about 1.031 ms/op / 3,107,786 B/op. The newest warmed JFR (`target/wp17-after-readpath.jfr`) now points to child indexing, join-state build cost, computed-field dependency lookup, and joined-row allocation as the next WP17 targets.

Updated `TODO.md` to match the landed WP17 work: current warmed baseline about 1.043 ms/op versus manual about 0.161 ms/op, current allocation baseline about 3,107,786 B/op, and the latest hotspot order led by `FastArrayQuerySupport.buildChildIndex`, `tryBuildJoinedState`, `ComputedFieldPlan.resolveValue`, and residual read-side reflection from `target/wp17-after-readpath.jfr`.

Continued WP17 by reducing join-build allocation on the selective single-join fast path: `FastArrayQuerySupport.buildChildIndex()` now stores single-child matches directly and only promotes to `List<Object[]>` on fan-out, while `tryBuildJoinedState()` reuses a single parent read buffer via the new `ReflectionUtil.readFlatRowValues(..., Object[], int)` helper. Focused tests and full `mvn -q test` passed, the rebuilt warmed 10k computed-field join rerun improved to about 0.654 ms/op versus the current manual baseline around 0.111 ms/op, the latest `-prof gc` rerun measured about 0.632 ms/op / 2,307,857 B/op, and the fresh warmed JFR (`target/wp17-after-parent-buffer.jfr`) now points first to `ReflectionUtil$ResolvedFieldPath.read` and `FastArrayQuerySupport$ComputedFieldPlan.resolveValue`.

Ran the full documented benchmark surface plus the standalone legacy PojoLensJmhBenchmark on 2026-03-14, wrote the detailed root report to BENCHMARKS.md, and added opt-in benchmark memory in `ai/state/benchmark-state.md` plus `ai/core/benchmark-context.md`. All core/chart threshold checks passed, chart parity failed in 5 of 15 fluent-vs-SQL-like comparisons, and the hotspot -prof gc run highlighted computed join selective materialization and reflection conversion as the main allocation stress points.

Broadened `TODO.md` from a WP17-only lens into a portfolio view backed by the 2026-03-14 benchmark sweep and recurring warmed JFR evidence. Added explicit work packages for chart parity and reflection/conversion hotspot reduction, recorded the cross-JFR hotspot clusters in `BENCHMARKS.md` plus benchmark AI memory, and updated hot state/handoff so future sessions treat `ReflectionUtil` and `FastArrayQuerySupport` as the common class-level stress points.

Continued WP17 by binding compiled numeric expressions to direct array indexes on the selective single-join fast path. `SqlExpressionEvaluator.CompiledExpression` now supports bound array evaluation and `FastArrayQuerySupport.applyComputedValues()` uses that bound form instead of per-row lambda/string dependency lookup. Focused tests and full `mvn -q test` passed, the rebuilt warmed 10k computed-field join rerun improved from about 0.654 ms/op to about 0.614 ms/op versus a current manual baseline around 0.108 ms/op, the latest `-prof gc` rerun stayed effectively flat at about 0.617 ms/op / 2,307,945 B/op with `gc.count` unchanged at 8 and `gc.time` up from 5 ms to 6 ms, and the fresh warmed JFR (`target/wp17-after-bound-expression.jfr`) now points first to `ReflectionUtil$ResolvedFieldPath.read`, `FastArrayQuerySupport.applyComputedValues`, `tryBuildJoinedState`, `buildChildIndex`, and projection writes.

Tried two WP17 follow-up directions on 2026-03-14 and reverted both on the same clean tree after regressions: compiled `ReflectionUtil` accessors and early join-time filtering/materialization in `FastArrayQuerySupport`. After the reverts, a fresh clean-tree long warmed rerun on the unchanged code measured about 0.892 ms/op versus a manual baseline around 0.111 ms/op, while the short allocation-focused rerun remained about 0.618 ms/op / 2,307,905 B/op with `gc.count` 8 and `gc.time` 5 ms. The next WP17 step should re-establish a controlled clean-tree repro/profile loop before trusting another optimization result.

Changed benchmark policy to stop treating chart fluent-vs-SQL-like parity as a gate. CI now keeps only chart threshold checks, `docs/benchmarking.md` and benchmark memory now judge chart/SQL-like work by absolute latency/allocation, `TODO.md` parks WP17 as good enough for now, promotes WP18 to absolute chart/SQL-like overhead work, and explicitly allows larger redesigns when they have the clearer path to benchmark wins.

Started WP18 runtime work by adding reusable prepared SQL-like execution shapes for non-subquery queries. `SqlLikeQuery` now caches validated/bound non-subquery shapes by source/projection/join schema, rebinds a request-scoped `FilterQueryBuilder` per execution, and avoids the extra `copyOnBuild` snapshot on the hot path; subquery-bearing SQL-like queries intentionally bypass that cache for correctness. `mvn -q test` passed, focused regression tests were added for repeated chart, join, and typed-bind executions, and a quick targeted JMH verification at `size=10000` measured `StatsQueryJmhBenchmark.sqlLikeParseAndTimeBucketMetricsToChart` at about `7.088 ms/op` versus `7.200 ms/op` for `fluentTimeBucketMetricsToChart`.

Continued WP18 by removing the typed projection round-trip from chart execution. `FilterImpl` and `SqlLikeQuery` now feed chart mapping directly from internal `QueryRow` results, `ChartMapper` uses indexed `QueryRow` field reads, and focused chart coverage was added for direct `QueryRow` inputs. `mvn -q test` and the focused chart tests passed, and exact targeted JMH reruns at `size=10000`, `-f 1 -wi 3 -i 5 -r 250ms` measured `StatsQueryJmhBenchmark.fluentTimeBucketMetricsToChart` at about `5.045 ms/op` and `sqlLikeParseAndTimeBucketMetricsToChart` at about `4.891 ms/op`, with matching query-only runs at about `4.990 ms/op` and `4.728 ms/op`.

Continued WP18 by removing the chart benchmark export hotspot. `ChartPayloadJsonExporter` now pre-sizes its buffer and appends fixed-scale numeric values directly instead of calling `String.format(...)` per point. `mvn -q test` passed, a rebuilt chart suite and strict threshold check still passed `45/45`, comparable cold reruns cut `ChartVisualizationJmhBenchmark.scatterPayloadJsonExport` from about `3.910/43.157/82.048 ms/op` to about `0.066/0.634/1.146 ms/op` at sizes `1000/10000/100000`, and a targeted `size=10000` `-prof gc` rerun measured about `0.367 ms/op` and `580,857 B/op`.

Regenerated affected AI indexes after adding `ChartPayloadJsonExporterTest` and rerunning the suite; `ai/indexes/files-index.json` and `ai/indexes/test-index.json` now record 64 test Java files, 60 test classes, and a passing `mvn -q test` run with 417 tests.

Continued WP18 with a consolidation pass on SQL-like raw-row stats execution. A broader attempt to route those paths through `FilterImpl` activated stats-plan cache reuse but regressed short 10k reruns to about `5.404 ms/op` and `5.034 ms/op`, so it was not kept. The landed narrower pass instead precomputes raw execution-plan cache keys on prepared non-join stats shapes; focused cache regressions were added for repeated aliased stats filter/chart execution, `mvn -q test` passed, and the resulting short 10k reruns stayed near baseline at about `4.889 ms/op` and `4.731 ms/op`.

Continued WP18 by consolidating `FilterQueryBuilder` snapshot construction so it no longer copies an already-copied `QuerySpec` a second time. A snapshot-isolation regression was added in `FilterQueryBuilderSelectiveMaterializationTest`, focused tests plus full `mvn -q test` passed, and rebuilt short 10k JMH reruns then drifted upward across both SQL-like and fluent controls (`6.418/6.632 ms/op` and `7.146/7.029 ms/op` respectively), so that session did not provide a trustworthy patch-specific speed signal.

Continued WP18 by replacing full builder cloning on the bean-backed prepared SQL-like path with a lighter `FilterQueryBuilder.preparedExecutionView(...)` that shares the validated query shape, while `QueryRow`-backed executions still fall back to isolated copies. `FilterImpl` and `SqlLikeQuery` now skip `RuleCleaner` on that validated path, `SqlLikeQueryContractTest` gained repeated bean-backed stats rebinding coverage, focused tests plus `mvn -q test` passed, a rebuilt benchmark runner succeeded, a narrow 10k-row rebinding probe measured about `4.228 us/op` for `preparedExecutionView(...)` versus `5.822 us/op` for `preparedExecutionCopy(...)`, and the matching short end-to-end JMH rerun stayed too drift-heavy (`7.510/6.783 ms/op` for SQL-like query/chart, `7.677/7.359 ms/op` for fluent) to cleanly attribute the remaining absolute cost.

Continued WP18 by extending the new direct bean-backed stats path through chart execution. `FastStatsQuerySupport` now stays in `Object[]` form for simple bean-backed non-join stats charts, `ChartMapper` gained indexed array-row mapping, `FilterImpl` and `SqlLikeQuery` route fast stats charts through that path, and `ChartMapperArrayRowsTest` pins the new mapper behavior. Focused chart/sql-like coverage plus full `mvn -q test` passed, and a rebuilt short 10k JMH rerun stayed mixed rather than attribution-clean: fluent query/chart measured about `6.934/6.336 ms/op`, while SQL-like query/chart measured about `6.318/7.173 ms/op`, so the next step is to profile the remaining SQL-like chart gap directly.

Continued WP18 with a setup-side refactor: `ReflectionUtil` now caches `FlatRowReadPlan` compilation by root type plus selected schema, and `SqlLikeQuery` now uses an explicit per-call execution run so filter/chart/raw-row paths reuse one rebound builder and one fast-stats resolution per invocation. `ReflectionUtilTest` gained read-plan cache coverage, `SqlLikeTypedBindContractTest` now covers repeated bound stats charts, focused regressions plus full `mvn -q test` passed, and a rebuilt short 10k JMH rerun stayed noisy overall but flipped SQL-like chart/query ordering in the same run (`7.033/6.381 ms/op`), which is weak setup-side evidence rather than a clean attribution result.
